name: wheel-stub
documentation:
# wheel-stub

This is a PEP 517 build backend to act as a "soft link" between a package released on a 3rd party package index and pypi.org. This allows users to keep the simple UX of `pip install foo` while hosting the actual wheel files on a non-PyPi index.

## Demo usage

1. Install hatch: https://hatch.pypa.io/latest/install/
2. Run `hatch shell`
3. Run `hatch build -t wheel`
4. `cd demo`
5. Grab your favorite wheel file on https://pypi.nvidia.com, e.g.
   ```
   wget https://pypi.nvidia.com/nvidia-cuda-runtime-cu12/nvidia_cuda_runtime_cu12-12.4.99-py3-none-manylinux2014_x86_64.whl
   ```
6. Build the sdist:
   ```
   PIP_FIND_LINKS=`pwd`/../dist python -m build --sdist --config-setting source_wheel=nvidia_cuda_runtime_cu12-12.4.99-py3-none-manylinux2014_x86_64.whl
   ```
   There should now be a new `dist/` folder with e.g. `nvidia_cuda_runtime_cu12-12.4.99.tar.gz`
8. Install the sdist:
   ```
   pip install --no-cache ./dist/<my_sdist>.tar.gz
   ```
   Feel free to test with `--extra-index-url https://pypi.nvidia.com` as well.
9. Done! You should see everything install correctly

## Configuration

There are a few configuration options available for `wheel_stub`. These can be set in your `pyproject.toml` file:

- `index_url`: The url to the 3rd party index to install wheels from. This is the only required configuration setting.

- `stub_only`: Do not install the wheel from the 3rd party package repository, instead erroring with instructions of how to use the repository directly. This can be used in cases such as a project which depends on non-PyPi dependencies, which would get rejected from pypi.org. Set automatically based on project dependencies. `true` or `false` (default `false`)

- `include_cuda_debuginfo`: Whether to query information about the available CUDA runtime when reporting an error. `true` or `false` (default `false`)

## License

This project is released under the Apache-2 license. It vendors the `packaging` project, which is BSD 2-clause and Apache 2 dual licensed. It also vendors `tomli`, which is MIT licensed. For more details see the [LICENSE](/LICENSE) file.

functions.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.utils.canonicalize_version
documentation:
This is very similar to Version.__str__, but has one subtle difference
with the way it handles the release segment.

classes.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.utils.InvalidName
documentation: An invalid distribution name; users should refer to the packaging user guide.

classes.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.utils.InvalidWheelFilename
documentation: An invalid wheel filename was found, users should refer to PEP 427.

classes.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.utils.InvalidSdistFilename
documentation: An invalid sdist filename was found, users should refer to the packaging user guide.

modules.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.markers
documentation: This file is dual licensed under the terms of the Apache License, Version
2.0, and the BSD License. See the LICENSE file in the root of this repository
for complete details.

functions.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.markers._normalize_extra_values
documentation: Normalize extra values.

classes.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.markers.InvalidMarker
documentation: An invalid marker was found, users should refer to PEP 508.

classes.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.markers.UndefinedComparison
documentation: An invalid operation was attempted on a value that doesn't support it.

classes.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.markers.UndefinedEnvironmentName
documentation:
A name was attempted to be used that does not exist inside of the
environment.

class_variables.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.markers.Environment.implementation_name
documentation: The implementation's identifier, e.g. `'cpython'`.

class_variables.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.markers.Environment.implementation_version
documentation:
The implementation's version, e.g. `'3.13.0a2'` for CPython 3.13.0a2, or
`'7.3.13'` for PyPy3.10 v7.3.13.

class_variables.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.markers.Environment.os_name
documentation:
The value of {py:data}`os.name`. The name of the operating system dependent module
imported, e.g. `'posix'`.

class_variables.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.markers.Environment.platform_machine
documentation:
Returns the machine type, e.g. `'i386'`.

An empty string if the value cannot be determined.

class_variables.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.markers.Environment.platform_release
documentation:
The system's release, e.g. `'2.2.0'` or `'NT'`.

An empty string if the value cannot be determined.

class_variables.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.markers.Environment.platform_system
documentation:
The system/OS name, e.g. `'Linux'`, `'Windows'` or `'Java'`.

An empty string if the value cannot be determined.

class_variables.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.markers.Environment.platform_version
documentation:
The system's release version, e.g. `'#3 on degas'`.

An empty string if the value cannot be determined.

class_variables.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.markers.Environment.python_full_version
documentation:
The Python version as string `'major.minor.patchlevel'`.

Note that unlike the Python {py:data}`sys.version`, this value will always include
the patchlevel (it defaults to 0).

class_variables.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.markers.Environment.platform_python_implementation
documentation: A string identifying the Python implementation, e.g. `'CPython'`.

class_variables.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.markers.Environment.python_version
documentation: The Python version as string `'major.minor'`.

class_variables.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.markers.Environment.sys_platform
documentation:
This string contains a platform identifier that can be used to append
platform-specific components to {py:data}`sys.path`, for instance.

For Unix systems, except on Linux and AIX, this is the lowercased OS name as
returned by `uname -s` with the first part of the version as returned by
`uname -r` appended, e.g. `'sunos5'` or `'freebsd8'`, at the time when Python
was built.

methods.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.markers.Marker.evaluate
documentation:
Evaluate a marker.

Return the boolean from evaluating the given marker against the
environment. environment is an optional argument to override all or
part of the determined environment.

The environment is determined from the current Python process.

modules.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging._parser
documentation:
Handwritten parser of dependency specifiers.

The docstring for each __parse_* function contains EBNF-inspired grammar representing
the implementation.

functions.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging._parser.parse_requirement
documentation: Recursive descent parser for dependency specifier

functions.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging._parser._parse_requirement
documentation: requirement = WS? IDENTIFIER WS? extras WS? requirement_details

functions.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging._parser._parse_requirement_details
documentation:
requirement_details = AT URL (WS requirement_marker?)?
                    | specifier WS? (requirement_marker)?

functions.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging._parser._parse_requirement_marker
documentation: requirement_marker = SEMICOLON marker WS?

functions.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging._parser._parse_extras
documentation: extras = (LEFT_BRACKET wsp* extras_list? wsp* RIGHT_BRACKET)?

functions.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging._parser._parse_extras_list
documentation: extras_list = identifier (wsp* ',' wsp* identifier)*

functions.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging._parser._parse_specifier
documentation:
specifier = LEFT_PARENTHESIS WS? version_many WS? RIGHT_PARENTHESIS
          | WS? version_many WS?

functions.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging._parser._parse_version_many
documentation: version_many = (SPECIFIER (WS? COMMA WS? SPECIFIER)*)?

functions.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging._parser.parse_marker
documentation: Recursive descent parser for marker expression

functions.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging._parser._parse_marker
documentation: marker = marker_atom (BOOLOP marker_atom)+

functions.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging._parser._parse_marker_atom
documentation:
marker_atom = WS? LEFT_PARENTHESIS WS? marker WS? RIGHT_PARENTHESIS WS?
            | WS? marker_item WS?

functions.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging._parser._parse_marker_item
documentation: marker_item = WS? marker_var WS? marker_op WS? marker_var WS?

functions.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging._parser._parse_marker_var
documentation: marker_var = VARIABLE | QUOTED_STRING

functions.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging._parser._parse_marker_op
documentation: marker_op = IN | NOT IN | OP

functions.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging._manylinux._parse_elf
documentation: `os.PathLike` not a generic type until Python 3.9, so sticking with `str`
as the type for `path` until then.

functions.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging._manylinux._glibc_version_string_confstr
documentation: Primary implementation of glibc_version_string using os.confstr.

functions.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging._manylinux._glibc_version_string_ctypes
documentation: Fallback implementation of glibc_version_string using ctypes.

functions.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging._manylinux._glibc_version_string
documentation: Returns glibc version string, or None if not using glibc.

functions.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging._manylinux._parse_glibc_version
documentation:
Parse glibc version.

We use a regexp instead of str.split because we want to discard any
random junk that might come after the minor version -- this might happen
in patched/forked versions of glibc (e.g. Linaro's version of glibc
uses version strings like "2.20-2014.11"). See gh-3588.

functions.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging._manylinux._is_compatible
documentation: From PEP 513, PEP 600

functions.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging._manylinux.platform_tags
documentation:
Generate manylinux tags compatible to the current platform.

```
param archs

Sequence of compatible architectures.
The first one shall be the closest to the actual architecture and be the part of
platform tag after the linux_ prefix, e.g. x86_64.
The linux_ prefix is assumed as a prerequisite for the current platform to
be manylinux-compatible.

returns

An iterator of compatible manylinux tags.
```

modules.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging._structures
documentation: This file is dual licensed under the terms of the Apache License, Version
2.0, and the BSD License. See the LICENSE file in the root of this repository
for complete details.

modules.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging._musllinux
documentation:
PEP 656 support.

This module implements logic to detect if the currently running Python is
linked against musl, and what musl version is used.

functions.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging._musllinux._get_musl_version
documentation:
Detect currently-running musl runtime version.

This is done by checking the specified executable's dynamic linking
information, and invoking the loader to parse its output for a version
string. If the loader is musl, the output would be something like:

```
musl libc (x86_64)
Version 1.2.2
Dynamic Program Loader
```

functions.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging._musllinux.platform_tags
documentation:
Generate musllinux tags compatible to the current platform.

```
param archs

Sequence of compatible architectures.
The first one shall be the closest to the actual architecture and be the part of
platform tag after the linux_ prefix, e.g. x86_64.
The linux_ prefix is assumed as a prerequisite for the current platform to
be musllinux-compatible.

returns

An iterator of compatible musllinux tags.
```

modules.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.specifiers
documentation:
```
.. testsetup::

    from packaging.specifiers import Specifier, SpecifierSet, InvalidSpecifier
    from packaging.version import Version
```
This file is dual licensed under the terms of the Apache License, Version
2.0, and the BSD License. See the LICENSE file in the root of this repository
for complete details.

functions.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.specifiers._version_split
documentation:
Split version into components.

The split components are intended for version comparison. The logic does
not attempt to retain the original version string, so joining the
components back with {func}`_version_join` may not produce the original
version string.

functions.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.specifiers._version_join
documentation:
Join split version components into a version string.

This function assumes the input came from {func}`_version_split`, where the
first component must be the epoch (either empty or numeric), and all other
components numeric.

classes.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.specifiers.InvalidSpecifier
documentation:
Raised when attempting to create a {class}`Specifier` with a specifier
string that is invalid.

```pycon
>>> Specifier("lolwat")
Traceback (most recent call last):
    ...
packaging.specifiers.InvalidSpecifier: Invalid specifier: 'lolwat'
```

methods.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.specifiers.BaseSpecifier.__str__
documentation:
Returns the str representation of this Specifier-like object. This
should be representative of the Specifier itself.

methods.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.specifiers.BaseSpecifier.__hash__
documentation: Returns a hash value for this Specifier-like object.

methods.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.specifiers.BaseSpecifier.__eq__
documentation:
Returns a boolean representing whether or not the two Specifier-like
objects are equal.

```
param other

The other object to check against.
```

methods.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.specifiers.BaseSpecifier.prereleases
documentation:
Whether or not pre-releases as a whole are allowed.

This can be set to either `True` or `False` to explicitly enable or disable
prereleases or it can be set to `None` (the default) to use default semantics.

methods.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.specifiers.BaseSpecifier.prereleases
documentation:
Setter for {attr}`prereleases`.

```
param value

The value to set.
```

methods.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.specifiers.BaseSpecifier.contains
documentation: Determines if the given item is contained within this specifier.

methods.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.specifiers.BaseSpecifier.filter
documentation:
Takes an iterable of items and filters them so that only items which
are contained within this specifier are allowed in it.

classes.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.specifiers.Specifier

methods.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.specifiers.Specifier.__init__
documentation:
Initialize a Specifier instance.

```
param spec

The string representation of a specifier which will be parsed and
normalized before use.

param prereleases

This tells the specifier if it should accept prerelease versions if
applicable or not. The default of None will autodetect it from the
given specifiers.

raises InvalidSpecifier

If the given specifier is invalid (i.e. bad syntax).
```

methods.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.specifiers.Specifier.prereleases
documentation: https://github.com/python/mypy/pull/13475#pullrequestreview-1079784515

methods.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.specifiers.Specifier.operator
documentation:
The operator of this specifier.

```pycon
>>> Specifier("==1.2.3").operator
'=='
```

methods.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.specifiers.Specifier.version
documentation:
The version of this specifier.

```pycon
>>> Specifier("==1.2.3").version
'1.2.3'
```

methods.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.specifiers.Specifier.__repr__
documentation:
A representation of the Specifier that shows all internal state.

```pycon
>>> Specifier('>=1.0.0')
<Specifier('>=1.0.0')>
>>> Specifier('>=1.0.0', prereleases=False)
<Specifier('>=1.0.0', prereleases=False)>
>>> Specifier('>=1.0.0', prereleases=True)
<Specifier('>=1.0.0', prereleases=True)>
```

methods.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.specifiers.Specifier.__str__
documentation:
A string representation of the Specifier that can be round-tripped.

```pycon
>>> str(Specifier('>=1.0.0'))
'>=1.0.0'
>>> str(Specifier('>=1.0.0', prereleases=False))
'>=1.0.0'
```

methods.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.specifiers.Specifier.__eq__
documentation:
Whether or not the two Specifier-like objects are equal.

```
param other

The other object to check against.
```

The value of {attr}`prereleases` is ignored.

```pycon
>>> Specifier("==1.2.3") == Specifier("== 1.2.3.0")
True
>>> (Specifier("==1.2.3", prereleases=False) ==
...  Specifier("==1.2.3", prereleases=True))
True
>>> Specifier("==1.2.3") == "==1.2.3"
True
>>> Specifier("==1.2.3") == Specifier("==1.2.4")
False
>>> Specifier("==1.2.3") == Specifier("~=1.2.3")
False
```

methods.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.specifiers.Specifier.__contains__
documentation:
Return whether or not the item is contained in this specifier.

```
param item

The item to check for.
```

This is used for the `in` operator and behaves the same as
{meth}`contains` with no `prereleases` argument passed.

```pycon
>>> "1.2.3" in Specifier(">=1.2.3")
True
>>> Version("1.2.3") in Specifier(">=1.2.3")
True
>>> "1.0.0" in Specifier(">=1.2.3")
False
>>> "1.3.0a1" in Specifier(">=1.2.3")
False
>>> "1.3.0a1" in Specifier(">=1.2.3", prereleases=True)
True
```

methods.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.specifiers.Specifier.contains
documentation:
Return whether or not the item is contained in this specifier.

```
param item

The item to check for, which can be a version string or a
 instance.

param prereleases

Whether or not to match prereleases with this Specifier. If set to
None (the default), it uses  to determine
whether or not prereleases are allowed.
```

```pycon
>>> Specifier(">=1.2.3").contains("1.2.3")
True
>>> Specifier(">=1.2.3").contains(Version("1.2.3"))
True
>>> Specifier(">=1.2.3").contains("1.0.0")
False
>>> Specifier(">=1.2.3").contains("1.3.0a1")
False
>>> Specifier(">=1.2.3", prereleases=True).contains("1.3.0a1")
True
>>> Specifier(">=1.2.3").contains("1.3.0a1", prereleases=True)
True
```

methods.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.specifiers.Specifier.filter
documentation:
Filter items in the given iterable, that match the specifier.

```
param iterable

An iterable that can contain version strings and  instances.
The items in the iterable will be filtered according to the specifier.

param prereleases

Whether or not to allow prereleases in the returned iterator. If set to
None (the default), it will be intelligently decide whether to allow
prereleases or not (based on the  attribute, and
whether the only versions matching are prereleases).
```

This method is smarter than just `filter(Specifier().contains, [...])`
because it implements the rule from {pep}`440` that a prerelease item
SHOULD be accepted if no other versions match the given specifier.

```pycon
>>> list(Specifier(">=1.2.3").filter(["1.2", "1.3", "1.5a1"]))
['1.3']
>>> list(Specifier(">=1.2.3").filter(["1.2", "1.2.3", "1.3", Version("1.4")]))
['1.2.3', '1.3', <Version('1.4')>]
>>> list(Specifier(">=1.2.3").filter(["1.2", "1.5a1"]))
['1.5a1']
>>> list(Specifier(">=1.2.3").filter(["1.3", "1.5a1"], prereleases=True))
['1.3', '1.5a1']
>>> list(Specifier(">=1.2.3", prereleases=True).filter(["1.3", "1.5a1"]))
['1.3', '1.5a1']
```

This class abstracts handling of version specifiers.

:::{tip}
It is generally not required to instantiate this manually. You should instead
prefer to work with {class}`SpecifierSet` instead, which can parse
comma-separated version specifiers (which is what package metadata contains).
:::

classes.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.specifiers.SpecifierSet

methods.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.specifiers.SpecifierSet.__init__
documentation:
Initialize a SpecifierSet instance.

```
param specifiers

The string representation of a specifier or a comma-separated list of
specifiers which will be parsed and normalized before use.

param prereleases

This tells the SpecifierSet if it should accept prerelease versions if
applicable or not. The default of None will autodetect it from the
given specifiers.

raises InvalidSpecifier

If the given specifiers are not parseable than this exception will be
raised.
```

methods.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.specifiers.SpecifierSet.__repr__
documentation:
A representation of the specifier set that shows all internal state.

Note that the ordering of the individual specifiers within the set may not
match the input string.

```pycon
>>> SpecifierSet('>=1.0.0,!=2.0.0')
<SpecifierSet('!=2.0.0,>=1.0.0')>
>>> SpecifierSet('>=1.0.0,!=2.0.0', prereleases=False)
<SpecifierSet('!=2.0.0,>=1.0.0', prereleases=False)>
>>> SpecifierSet('>=1.0.0,!=2.0.0', prereleases=True)
<SpecifierSet('!=2.0.0,>=1.0.0', prereleases=True)>
```

methods.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.specifiers.SpecifierSet.__str__
documentation:
A string representation of the specifier set that can be round-tripped.

Note that the ordering of the individual specifiers within the set may not
match the input string.

```pycon
>>> str(SpecifierSet(">=1.0.0,!=1.0.1"))
'!=1.0.1,>=1.0.0'
>>> str(SpecifierSet(">=1.0.0,!=1.0.1", prereleases=False))
'!=1.0.1,>=1.0.0'
```

methods.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.specifiers.SpecifierSet.__and__
documentation:
Return a SpecifierSet which is a combination of the two sets.

```
param other

The other object to combine with.
```

```pycon
>>> SpecifierSet(">=1.0.0,!=1.0.1") & '<=2.0.0,!=2.0.1'
<SpecifierSet('!=1.0.1,!=2.0.1,<=2.0.0,>=1.0.0')>
>>> SpecifierSet(">=1.0.0,!=1.0.1") & SpecifierSet('<=2.0.0,!=2.0.1')
<SpecifierSet('!=1.0.1,!=2.0.1,<=2.0.0,>=1.0.0')>
```

methods.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.specifiers.SpecifierSet.__eq__
documentation:
Whether or not the two SpecifierSet-like objects are equal.

```
param other

The other object to check against.
```

The value of {attr}`prereleases` is ignored.

```pycon
>>> SpecifierSet(">=1.0.0,!=1.0.1") == SpecifierSet(">=1.0.0,!=1.0.1")
True
>>> (SpecifierSet(">=1.0.0,!=1.0.1", prereleases=False) ==
...  SpecifierSet(">=1.0.0,!=1.0.1", prereleases=True))
True
>>> SpecifierSet(">=1.0.0,!=1.0.1") == ">=1.0.0,!=1.0.1"
True
>>> SpecifierSet(">=1.0.0,!=1.0.1") == SpecifierSet(">=1.0.0")
False
>>> SpecifierSet(">=1.0.0,!=1.0.1") == SpecifierSet(">=1.0.0,!=1.0.2")
False
```

methods.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.specifiers.SpecifierSet.__len__
documentation: Returns the number of specifiers in this specifier set.

methods.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.specifiers.SpecifierSet.__iter__
documentation:
Returns an iterator over all the underlying {class}`Specifier` instances
in this specifier set.

```pycon
>>> sorted(SpecifierSet(">=1.0.0,!=1.0.1"), key=str)
[<Specifier('!=1.0.1')>, <Specifier('>=1.0.0')>]
```

methods.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.specifiers.SpecifierSet.__contains__
documentation:
Return whether or not the item is contained in this specifier.

```
param item

The item to check for.
```

This is used for the `in` operator and behaves the same as
{meth}`contains` with no `prereleases` argument passed.

```pycon
>>> "1.2.3" in SpecifierSet(">=1.0.0,!=1.0.1")
True
>>> Version("1.2.3") in SpecifierSet(">=1.0.0,!=1.0.1")
True
>>> "1.0.1" in SpecifierSet(">=1.0.0,!=1.0.1")
False
>>> "1.3.0a1" in SpecifierSet(">=1.0.0,!=1.0.1")
False
>>> "1.3.0a1" in SpecifierSet(">=1.0.0,!=1.0.1", prereleases=True)
True
```

methods.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.specifiers.SpecifierSet.contains
documentation:
Return whether or not the item is contained in this SpecifierSet.

```
param item

The item to check for, which can be a version string or a
 instance.

param prereleases

Whether or not to match prereleases with this SpecifierSet. If set to
None (the default), it uses  to determine
whether or not prereleases are allowed.
```

```pycon
>>> SpecifierSet(">=1.0.0,!=1.0.1").contains("1.2.3")
True
>>> SpecifierSet(">=1.0.0,!=1.0.1").contains(Version("1.2.3"))
True
>>> SpecifierSet(">=1.0.0,!=1.0.1").contains("1.0.1")
False
>>> SpecifierSet(">=1.0.0,!=1.0.1").contains("1.3.0a1")
False
>>> SpecifierSet(">=1.0.0,!=1.0.1", prereleases=True).contains("1.3.0a1")
True
>>> SpecifierSet(">=1.0.0,!=1.0.1").contains("1.3.0a1", prereleases=True)
True
```

methods.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.specifiers.SpecifierSet.filter
documentation:
Filter items in the given iterable, that match the specifiers in this set.

```
param iterable

An iterable that can contain version strings and  instances.
The items in the iterable will be filtered according to the specifier.

param prereleases

Whether or not to allow prereleases in the returned iterator. If set to
None (the default), it will be intelligently decide whether to allow
prereleases or not (based on the  attribute, and
whether the only versions matching are prereleases).
```

This method is smarter than just `filter(SpecifierSet(...).contains, [...])`
because it implements the rule from {pep}`440` that a prerelease item
SHOULD be accepted if no other versions match the given specifier.

```pycon
>>> list(SpecifierSet(">=1.2.3").filter(["1.2", "1.3", "1.5a1"]))
['1.3']
>>> list(SpecifierSet(">=1.2.3").filter(["1.2", "1.3", Version("1.4")]))
['1.3', <Version('1.4')>]
>>> list(SpecifierSet(">=1.2.3").filter(["1.2", "1.5a1"]))
[]
>>> list(SpecifierSet(">=1.2.3").filter(["1.3", "1.5a1"], prereleases=True))
['1.3', '1.5a1']
>>> list(SpecifierSet(">=1.2.3", prereleases=True).filter(["1.3", "1.5a1"]))
['1.3', '1.5a1']
```

An "empty" SpecifierSet will filter items based on the presence of prerelease
versions in the set.

```pycon
>>> list(SpecifierSet("").filter(["1.3", "1.5a1"]))
['1.3']
>>> list(SpecifierSet("").filter(["1.5a1"]))
['1.5a1']
>>> list(SpecifierSet("", prereleases=True).filter(["1.3", "1.5a1"]))
['1.3', '1.5a1']
>>> list(SpecifierSet("").filter(["1.3", "1.5a1"], prereleases=True))
['1.3', '1.5a1']
```

This class abstracts handling of a set of version specifiers.

It can be passed a single specifier (`>=3.0`), a comma-separated list of
specifiers (`>=3.0,!=3.1`), or no specifier at all.

Based on: <https://gist.github.com/lyssdod/f51579ae8d93c8657a5564aefc2ffbca>

methods.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging._tokenizer.Tokenizer.check

Check whether the next token has the provided name.

By default, if the check succeeds, the token *must* be read before
another check. If `peek` is set to `True`, the token is not loaded and
would need to be checked again.

Provides methods to examine the input stream to check whether the next token
matches.

functions.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.tags.parse_tag
documentation:
Parses the provided tag (e.g. `py3-none-any`) into a frozenset of Tag instances.

Returning a set is required due to the possibility that the tag is a
compressed tag set.

functions.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.tags._is_threaded_cpython
documentation:
Determine if the ABI corresponds to a threaded (`--disable-gil`) build.

The threaded builds are indicated by a "t" in the abiflags.

functions.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.tags._abi3_applies
documentation:
Determine if the Python version supports abi3.

PEP 384 was first implemented in Python 3.2. The threaded (`--disable-gil`)
builds do not support abi3.

functions.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.tags.cpython_tags
documentation:
Yields the tags for a CPython interpreter.

The tags consist of:
- cp<python_version>-<abi>-<platform>
- cp<python_version>-abi3-<platform>
- cp<python_version>-none-<platform>
- cp<less than python_version>-abi3-<platform>  # Older Python versions down to 3.2.

If python_version only specifies a major version then user-provided ABIs and
the 'none' ABItag will be used.

If 'abi3' or 'none' are specified in 'abis' then they will be yielded at
their normal position and not at the beginning.

functions.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.tags._generic_abi
documentation: Return the ABI tag based on EXT_SUFFIX.

functions.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.tags.generic_tags
documentation:
Yields the tags for a generic interpreter.

The tags consist of:
- <interpreter>-<abi>-<platform>

The "none" ABI will be added if it was not explicitly provided.

functions.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.tags._py_interpreter_range
documentation:
Yields Python versions in descending order.

After the latest version, the major-only version will be yielded, and then
all previous versions of that major version.

functions.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.tags.compatible_tags
documentation:
Yields the sequence of tags that are compatible with a specific version of Python.

The tags consist of:
- py*-none-<platform>
- <interpreter>-none-any  # ... if `interpreter` is provided.
- py*-none-any

functions.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.tags.mac_platforms
documentation:
Yields the platform tags for a macOS system.

The `version` parameter is a two-item tuple specifying the macOS version to
generate platform tags for. The `arch` parameter is the CPU architecture to
generate platform tags for. Both parameters default to the appropriate value
for the current system.

functions.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.tags.platform_tags
documentation: Provides the platform tags for this installation.

functions.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.tags.interpreter_name
documentation:
Returns the name of the running interpreter.

Some implementations have a reserved, two-letter abbreviation which will
be returned when appropriate.

functions.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.tags.interpreter_version
documentation: Returns the version of the running interpreter.

functions.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.tags.sys_tags
documentation:
Returns the sequence of tag triples for the running interpreter.

The order of the sequence corresponds to priority order for the
interpreter, from most to least important.

classes.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.tags.Tag
documentation:
A representation of the tag triple for a wheel.

Instances are considered immutable and thus are hashable. Equality checking
is also supported.

functions.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.metadata.parse_email
documentation:
Parse a distribution's metadata stored as email headers (e.g. from `METADATA`).

This function returns a two-item tuple of dicts. The first dict is of
recognized fields from the core metadata specification. Fields that can be
parsed and translated into Python's built-in types are converted
appropriately. All other fields are left as-is. Fields that are allowed to
appear multiple times are stored as lists.

The second dict contains all other fields from the metadata. This includes
any unrecognized fields. It also includes any fields which are expected to
be parsed into a built-in type but were not formatted appropriately. Finally,
any fields that are expected to appear only once but are repeated are
included in this dict.

classes.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.metadata.ExceptionGroup
documentation:
A minimal implementation of {external:exc}`ExceptionGroup` from Python 3.11.

If {external:exc}`ExceptionGroup` is already defined by Python itself,
that version is used instead.

classes.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.metadata.InvalidMetadata

class_variables.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.metadata.InvalidMetadata.field
documentation: The name of the field that contains invalid data.
A metadata field contains invalid data.

classes.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.metadata.RawMetadata
documentation:
A dictionary of raw core metadata.

Each field in core metadata maps to a key of this dictionary (when data is
provided). The key is lower-case and underscores are used instead of dashes
compared to the equivalent core metadata field. Any core metadata field that
can be specified multiple times or can hold multiple values in a single
field have a key with a plural name. See {class}`Metadata` whose attributes
match the keys of this dictionary.

Core metadata fields that can be specified multiple times are stored as a
list or dict depending on which is appropriate for the field. Any fields
which hold multiple values in a single field are stored as a list.
The RawMetadata class attempts to make as few assumptions about the underlying
serialization formats as possible. The idea is that as long as a serialization
formats offer some very basic primitives in *some* way then we can support
serializing to and from that format.

classes.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.metadata._Validator

methods.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.metadata._Validator._process_summary
documentation: Check the field contains no newlines.

Validate a metadata field.

All \_process\_\*() methods correspond to a core metadata field. The method is
called with the field's raw value. If the raw value is valid it is returned
in its "enriched" form (e.g. `version.Version` for the `Version` field).
If the raw value is invalid, {exc}`InvalidMetadata` is raised (with a cause
as appropriate).

{py:attr}`~BaseException.__cause__` attribute as appropriate).

documentation:
A string containing the regular expression used to match a valid version.

The pattern is not anchored at either end, and is intended for embedding in larger
expressions (for example, matching a version number as part of a file name). The
regular expression should be compiled with the `re.VERBOSE` and `re.IGNORECASE`
flags set.

```

<Version('1.0.0')>
```

methods.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.version.Version.__str__
documentation:

```pycon

```

methods.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.version.Version.epoch
documentation:
The epoch of the version.

```pycon
>>> Version("2.0.0").epoch
0
>>> Version("1!2.0.0").epoch
1
```

methods.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.version.Version.release
documentation:
The components of the "release" segment of the version.

```pycon
>>> Version("1.2.3").release
(1, 2, 3)
>>> Version("2.0.0").release
(2, 0, 0)
>>> Version("1!2.0.0.post0").release
(2, 0, 0)
```

Includes trailing zeroes but not the epoch or any pre-release / development /
post-release suffixes.

methods.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.version.Version.pre
documentation:
The pre-release segment of the version.

```pycon
>>> print(Version("1.2.3").pre)
None
>>> Version("1.2.3a1").pre
('a', 1)
>>> Version("1.2.3b1").pre
('b', 1)
>>> Version("1.2.3rc1").pre
('rc', 1)
```

methods.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.version.Version.post
documentation:
The post-release number of the version.

```pycon
>>> print(Version("1.2.3").post)
None
>>> Version("1.2.3.post1").post
1
```

methods.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.version.Version.dev
documentation:
The development number of the version.

```pycon
>>> print(Version("1.2.3").dev)
None
>>> Version("1.2.3.dev1").dev
1
```

methods.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.version.Version.local
documentation:
The local version segment of the version.

```pycon
>>> print(Version("1.2.3").local)
None
>>> Version("1.2.3+abc").local
'abc'
```

methods.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.version.Version.public
documentation:
The public portion of the version.

```pycon
>>> Version("1.2.3").public
'1.2.3'
>>> Version("1.2.3+abc").public
'1.2.3'
>>> Version("1.2.3+abc.dev1").public
'1.2.3'
```

methods.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.version.Version.base_version
documentation:
The "base version" of the version.

```pycon
>>> Version("1.2.3").base_version
'1.2.3'
>>> Version("1.2.3+abc").base_version
'1.2.3'
>>> Version("1!1.2.3+abc.dev1").base_version
'1!1.2.3'
```

The "base version" is the public version of the project without any pre or post
release markers.

methods.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.version.Version.is_prerelease
documentation:
Whether this version is a pre-release.

```pycon
>>> Version("1.2.3").is_prerelease
False
>>> Version("1.2.3a1").is_prerelease
True
>>> Version("1.2.3b1").is_prerelease
True
>>> Version("1.2.3rc1").is_prerelease
True
>>> Version("1.2.3dev1").is_prerelease
True
```

methods.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.version.Version.is_postrelease
documentation:
Whether this version is a post-release.

```pycon
>>> Version("1.2.3").is_postrelease
False
>>> Version("1.2.3.post1").is_postrelease
True
```

methods.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.version.Version.is_devrelease
documentation:
Whether this version is a development release.

```pycon
>>> Version("1.2.3").is_devrelease
False
>>> Version("1.2.3.dev1").is_devrelease
True
```

methods.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.version.Version.major
documentation:
The first item of {attr}`release` or `0` if unavailable.

```pycon
>>> Version("1.2.3").major
1
```

methods.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.version.Version.minor
documentation:
The second item of {attr}`release` or `0` if unavailable.

```pycon
>>> Version("1.2.3").minor
2
>>> Version("1").minor
0
```

methods.name: wheel_stub._vendor.packaging.version.Version.micro
documentation:
The third item of {attr}`release` or `0` if unavailable.

```pycon
>>> Version("1.2.3").micro
3
>>> Version("1").micro
0
```

This class abstracts handling of a project's versions.

A {class}`Version` instance is comparison aware and can be compared and
sorted using the standard Python interfaces.

```pycon
>>> v1 = Version("1.0a5")
>>> v2 = Version("1.0")
>>> v1
<Version('1.0a5')>
>>> v2
<Version('1.0')>
>>> v1 < v2
True
>>> v1 == v2
False
>>> v1 > v2
False
>>> v1 >= v2
False
>>> v1 <= v2
True
```

functions.name: wheel_stub._vendor.tomli._parser.load
documentation: Parse TOML from a binary file object.

documentation: Parse TOML from a string.

functions.name: wheel_stub._vendor.tomli._parser.suffixed_err
documentation:
Return a `TOMLDecodeError` where error message is suffixed with
coordinates in source.

functions.name: wheel_stub._vendor.tomli._parser.make_safe_parse_float
documentation:
A decorator to make `parse_float` safe.

`parse_float` must not return dicts or lists, because these types
would be mixed with parsed TOML tables and arrays, thus confusing
the parser. The returned decorated callable raises `ValueError`
instead of returning illegal types.

classes.name: wheel_stub._vendor.tomli._parser.TOMLDecodeError
documentation: An error raised if a document is not valid TOML.
